contributions.yaml_content:
You are generating an evidence triage policy YAML for a Wire Node knowledge pyramid.

An evidence policy tells the pyramid how to handle "evidence" — the supporting
material attached to questions during and after a build. The three core levers
are: triage rules (when to answer, defer, or skip an evidence request), demand
signals (thresholds that flip a deferred question back on), and budget (which
model tiers and concurrency limits apply to evidence work).

Convert the user's natural-language intent into a valid `evidence_policy` YAML
conforming to the JSON Schema below. Keep the document minimal — only include
fields the user's intent motivates. Omit optional sections when they have no
explicit driver in the intent.

## Schema (JSON Schema)
{schema}

## User Intent
{intent}

{if current_yaml}
## Current Values (you are refining this existing policy)
{current_yaml}
{end}

{if notes}
## User Refinement Notes
{notes}

Apply these notes to the current values. Keep everything else the same unless
the notes imply changes. Preserve the user's intent from prior rounds.
{end}

## Output rules

- Output ONLY the YAML document, no prose before or after
- Start the document with `schema_type: evidence_policy`
- Include brief inline comments (`# ...`) explaining non-obvious choices,
  especially where the intent or notes drove a specific value
- Use the enum values exactly as they appear in the schema (e.g. `answer`,
  `defer`, `skip`, `normal`, `high`, `low`)
- Prefer conservative defaults when the intent is ambiguous — users can always
  refine via notes later
contributions.triggering_note: Bundled generation prompt for evidence_policy (wire-node v0.2.0)

contributions.yaml_content: {"$schema":"http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#","title":"Evidence Policy","type":"object","required":["schema_type"],"properties":{"schema_type":{"type":"string","const":"evidence_policy"},"triage_rules":{"type":"array","items":{"type":"object","required":["condition","action"],"properties":{"condition":{"type":"string"},"action":{"type":"string","enum":["answer","defer","skip"]},"model_tier":{"type":"string"},"priority":{"type":"string","enum":["normal","high","low"]},"check_interval":{"type":"string"}}}},"demand_signals":{"type":"array","items":{"type":"object","required":["type","threshold","window"],"properties":{"type":{"type":"string"},"threshold":{"type":"integer","minimum":1},"window":{"type":"string"}}}},"budget":{"type":"object","properties":{"maintenance_model_tier":{"type":"string"},"initial_build_model_tier":{"type":"string"},"max_concurrent_evidence":{"type":"integer","minimum":1},"triage_batch_size":{"type":"integer","minimum":1}}}}}
contributions.triggering_note: Bundled JSON schema for evidence_policy (wire-node v0.2.0)

contributions.yaml_content:
schema_type: evidence_policy
applies_to: evidence_policy
version: 1
label: "Evidence Triage Policy"
description: "How the pyramid decides which evidence requests to answer, defer, or skip"
fields:
  triage_rules:
    label: "Triage Rules"
    help: "Ordered rules that decide what to do with each evidence request"
    widget: code
    visibility: advanced
  demand_signals:
    label: "Demand Signals"
    help: "Thresholds that flip deferred questions back on when they gain traction"
    widget: code
    visibility: advanced
  budget.maintenance_model_tier:
    label: "Maintenance Model Tier"
    help: "Tier used for ongoing maintenance evidence work"
    widget: select
    options_from: tier_registry
    visibility: basic
  budget.initial_build_model_tier:
    label: "Initial Build Model Tier"
    help: "Tier used for first-time evidence work on fresh pyramids"
    widget: select
    options_from: tier_registry
    visibility: basic
  budget.max_concurrent_evidence:
    label: "Max Concurrent Evidence"
    help: "Parallel evidence calls cap"
    widget: number
    min: 1
    max: 16
    step: 1
    visibility: basic
  budget.triage_batch_size:
    label: "Triage Batch Size"
    help: "Questions per triage LLM call"
    widget: number
    min: 1
    max: 64
    step: 1
    visibility: advanced
contributions.triggering_note: Bundled UI annotation for evidence_policy (wire-node v0.2.0)

contributions.yaml_content:
schema_type: evidence_policy
triage_rules:
  - condition: "first_build AND depth == 0"
    action: answer
    model_tier: stale_local
    priority: normal
  - condition: "stale_check AND no_demand_signals"
    action: defer
    check_interval: "never"
  - condition: "stale_check AND has_demand_signals"
    action: answer
    model_tier: stale_local
demand_signals:
  - type: agent_query_count
    threshold: 2
    window: "14d"
  - type: user_drill_count
    threshold: 1
    window: "7d"
budget:
  maintenance_model_tier: stale_local
  initial_build_model_tier: stale_local
  max_concurrent_evidence: 1
  triage_batch_size: 15
contributions.triggering_note: Bundled default evidence_policy (wire-node v0.2.0)

contributions.yaml_content:
You are generating an LLM dispatch policy YAML for a Wire Node knowledge pyramid.

A dispatch policy tells the node how to route each LLM call to providers. The
walker (`call_model_unified_with_audit_and_ctx`) iterates a rule's `route_to`
list in order and tries each entry: `market` (Wire compute market), `fleet`
(peer nodes), `openrouter` (cloud), `ollama-local` (local GPU), or any
registered provider id. On saturation or a RouteSkipped failure, walker
advances to the next entry. First successful response wins.

Key levers:
- `provider_pools`: per-provider concurrency + optional rate limit (sliding
  window). `openrouter` defaults to 20 concurrent / 60 rpm; `ollama-local` to
  concurrency 1 (single GPU).
- `routing_rules`: ordered list of `match_config` → `route_to` chains. First
  matching rule wins. `match_config` fields (work_type / min_depth /
  step_pattern) are ANDed; absent fields are wildcards.
- `route_to[*].max_budget_credits`: optional per-entry cost ceiling (credits).
  Walker passes this as `/quote` `max_budget` for the `market` branch. Absent
  means no cap (sentinel). Only meaningful on market entries today.
- `route_to[*].is_local`: true for providers that run on this node's hardware.
  Used by fleet receive-side to find which local provider to run a rule with.

Convert the user's natural-language intent into a valid `dispatch_policy` YAML
conforming to the JSON Schema below. Keep the document minimal — only include
fields the user's intent motivates. The single `default` rule with a
`market → fleet → openrouter → ollama-local` chain is the intended baseline;
operator intent usually reorders or adds rules rather than replacing the base.

## Schema (JSON Schema)
{schema}

## User Intent
{intent}

{if current_yaml}
## Current Values (you are refining this existing policy)
{current_yaml}
{end}

{if notes}
## User Refinement Notes
{notes}

Apply these notes to the current values. Keep everything else the same unless
the notes imply changes.
{end}

## Output rules

- Output ONLY the YAML document, no prose before or after
- Start the document with `version: 1`
- Use provider_id values exactly as the user references them; common ones are
  `market`, `fleet`, `openrouter`, `ollama-local`
- Include brief inline comments (`# ...`) explaining non-obvious choices
- Omit `max_budget_credits` unless the user explicitly asks for a cost cap
- Omit `escalation` — walker advances immediately on saturation; there is no
  wait-for-capacity semantic in rev 2.1
contributions.triggering_note: Bundled generation prompt for dispatch_policy (wire-node v0.2.0)

contributions.yaml_content: {"$schema":"http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#","title":"Dispatch Policy","type":"object","required":["version"],"properties":{"version":{"type":"integer","const":1},"provider_pools":{"type":"object","additionalProperties":{"type":"object","required":["concurrency"],"properties":{"concurrency":{"type":"integer","minimum":1},"rate_limit":{"type":"object","required":["max_requests","window_secs"],"properties":{"max_requests":{"type":"integer","minimum":1},"window_secs":{"type":"number","minimum":0}}}}}},"routing_rules":{"type":"array","items":{"type":"object","required":["name","match_config","route_to"],"properties":{"name":{"type":"string"},"match_config":{"type":"object","properties":{"work_type":{"type":"string","enum":["build","maintenance","evidence","faq","interactive","speculative"]},"min_depth":{"type":"integer"},"step_pattern":{"type":"string"}}},"route_to":{"type":"array","items":{"type":"object","required":["provider_id"],"properties":{"provider_id":{"type":"string"},"model_id":{"type":"string"},"tier_name":{"type":"string"},"is_local":{"type":"boolean"},"max_budget_credits":{"type":"integer","minimum":0,"description":"Per-entry cost ceiling in credits. Walker passes this as /quote max_budget for the market branch. Absent (None) resolves to NO_BUDGET_CAP sentinel (effectively unlimited)."}}}},"bypass_pool":{"type":"boolean"},"sequential":{"type":"boolean"}}}},"escalation":{"type":"object","properties":{"wait_timeout_secs":{"type":"integer","minimum":0},"max_wait_secs":{"type":"integer","minimum":0}}},"build_coordination":{"type":"object","properties":{"folder_builds_sequential":{"type":"boolean"},"defer_maintenance_during_build":{"type":"boolean"},"defer_dadbear_during_build":{"type":"boolean"}}},"max_batch_cost_usd":{"type":"number","minimum":0},"max_daily_cost_usd":{"type":"number","minimum":0}}}
contributions.triggering_note: Bundled JSON schema for dispatch_policy (wire-node v0.2.0)

contributions.yaml_content:
schema_type: schema_annotation
applies_to: dispatch_policy
version: 1
label: "LLM Dispatch Policy"
description: "How the node routes each LLM call across market, fleet, cloud, and local providers"
fields:
  provider_pools:
    label: "Provider Pools"
    help: "Per-provider concurrency and rate limits. Keys are provider ids (e.g. openrouter, ollama-local)."
    widget: code
    visibility: advanced
  routing_rules:
    label: "Routing Rules"
    help: "Ordered match-and-route rules. First matching rule wins; walker tries route_to entries in order."
    widget: code
    visibility: advanced
  routing_rules[].name:
    label: "Rule Name"
    help: "Human-readable identifier for this rule"
    widget: text
    visibility: basic
  routing_rules[].match_config.work_type:
    label: "Work Type"
    help: "Restrict this rule to a specific kind of LLM call (build, maintenance, evidence, faq, interactive, speculative). Omit for wildcard."
    widget: select
    options: [build, maintenance, evidence, faq, interactive, speculative]
    visibility: basic
  routing_rules[].match_config.min_depth:
    label: "Minimum Layer Depth"
    help: "Only apply this rule when the pyramid layer depth is >= this value"
    widget: number
    min: 0
    visibility: advanced
  routing_rules[].match_config.step_pattern:
    label: "Step Pattern"
    help: "Glob on step name; trailing * is supported (e.g. summarize_*)"
    widget: text
    visibility: advanced
  routing_rules[].route_to:
    label: "Provider Chain"
    help: "Ordered preference: walker tries each entry; advances on saturation or skippable failure. First success wins."
    widget: code
    visibility: basic
  routing_rules[].route_to[].provider_id:
    label: "Provider"
    help: "Provider id: market, fleet, openrouter, ollama-local, or any registered provider"
    widget: text
    visibility: basic
  routing_rules[].route_to[].model_id:
    label: "Model Override"
    help: "Override the provider default model for this entry. Optional."
    widget: text
    visibility: advanced
  routing_rules[].route_to[].tier_name:
    label: "Tier Name"
    help: "Optional tier name for context_limit / pricing lookups"
    widget: text
    visibility: advanced
  routing_rules[].route_to[].is_local:
    label: "Is Local Provider"
    help: "True when this provider runs on this node's hardware. Used by fleet receive-side to find which local provider to invoke when serving a peer's rule."
    widget: boolean
    visibility: advanced
  routing_rules[].route_to[].max_budget_credits:
    label: "Max Budget (credits)"
    help: "Per-entry cost ceiling passed to Wire /quote as max_budget. Only meaningful on the market entry. Leave blank for no cap."
    widget: number
    min: 0
    visibility: advanced
  routing_rules[].bypass_pool:
    label: "Bypass Pool Semaphore"
    help: "When true, skip the per-provider concurrency semaphore for this rule"
    widget: boolean
    visibility: advanced
  routing_rules[].sequential:
    label: "Serialize Calls"
    help: "When true, calls matching this rule run one at a time"
    widget: boolean
    visibility: advanced
  build_coordination.folder_builds_sequential:
    label: "Sequential Folder Builds"
    help: "Run folder builds one at a time instead of concurrently"
    widget: boolean
    visibility: basic
  build_coordination.defer_maintenance_during_build:
    label: "Pause Maintenance During Builds"
    help: "Defer stale checks while an active build is running"
    widget: boolean
    visibility: basic
  build_coordination.defer_dadbear_during_build:
    label: "Pause Auto-Update During Builds"
    help: "Defer DADBEAR self-update while an active build is running"
    widget: boolean
    visibility: basic
  max_batch_cost_usd:
    label: "Max Batch Cost (USD)"
    help: "Auto-commit ceiling per batch. Batches above this need manual approval."
    widget: number
    min: 0
    visibility: advanced
  max_daily_cost_usd:
    label: "Max Daily Cost (USD)"
    help: "Daily cap per slug. When cumulative spend plus preview cost exceeds this, a cost_limit hold is placed."
    widget: number
    min: 0
    visibility: advanced
contributions.triggering_note: Bundled UI annotation for dispatch_policy (wire-node v0.2.0)
Bundled default dispatch_policy (wire-node v0.2.0)

contributions.yaml_content:
You are generating a build strategy YAML for a Wire Node knowledge pyramid.

A build strategy tells the pyramid how to spend compute during its two primary
build phases: initial_build (first-time pyramid construction) and maintenance
(ongoing updates when source material changes). Each phase picks a model tier,
concurrency level, evidence depth, and whether to run webbing (cross-node
relationship discovery). The `quality` block gates what counts as a valid
pyramid output.

Convert the user's natural-language intent into a valid `build_strategy` YAML
conforming to the JSON Schema below. The user's intent typically covers trade-
offs: more thorough vs faster, local-only vs cloud, shallow vs deep evidence,
strict vs lenient quality gates.

## Schema (JSON Schema)
{schema}

## User Intent
{intent}

{if current_yaml}
## Current Values (you are refining this existing strategy)
{current_yaml}
{end}

{if notes}
## User Refinement Notes
{notes}

Apply these notes to the current values. Keep everything else the same unless
the notes imply changes.
{end}

## Output rules

- Output ONLY the YAML document, no prose before or after
- Start the document with `schema_type: build_strategy`
- Use the tier names the user references (e.g. `synth_heavy`, `stale_local`,
  `fast_extract`) — do NOT invent tier names; the user can edit them later
- Include inline comments explaining why a tier or concurrency value was chosen
- Prefer `evidence_mode: deep` for first builds unless the user explicitly asks
  for a lighter touch; prefer `evidence_mode: demand_only` for maintenance
contributions.triggering_note: Bundled generation prompt for build_strategy (wire-node v0.2.0)

contributions.yaml_content: {"$schema":"http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#","title":"Build Strategy","type":"object","required":["schema_type"],"properties":{"schema_type":{"type":"string","const":"build_strategy"},"initial_build":{"type":"object","properties":{"model_tier":{"type":"string"},"concurrency":{"type":"integer","minimum":1},"evidence_mode":{"type":"string","enum":["deep","shallow","skip","demand_only"]},"webbing":{"type":"boolean"}}},"maintenance":{"type":"object","properties":{"model_tier":{"type":"string"},"concurrency":{"type":"integer","minimum":1},"evidence_mode":{"type":"string","enum":["deep","shallow","skip","demand_only"]},"webbing":{"type":"boolean"}}},"quality":{"type":"object","properties":{"min_distillation_length":{"type":"integer","minimum":0},"require_evidence":{"type":"boolean"},"require_webbing":{"type":"boolean"}}}}}
contributions.triggering_note: Bundled JSON schema for build_strategy (wire-node v0.2.0)

contributions.yaml_content:
schema_type: build_strategy
applies_to: build_strategy
version: 1
label: "Build Strategy"
description: "How the pyramid spends compute across initial build + maintenance phases"
fields:
  initial_build.model_tier:
    label: "Initial Build Tier"
    help: "Model tier used for first-time pyramid construction"
    widget: select
    options_from: tier_registry
    visibility: basic
  initial_build.concurrency:
    label: "Initial Build Concurrency"
    help: "Parallel chain steps during initial build"
    widget: number
    min: 1
    max: 64
    step: 1
    visibility: basic
  initial_build.evidence_mode:
    label: "Initial Evidence Mode"
    help: "How much evidence work to do during first build"
    widget: select
    options:
      - value: deep
        label: "Deep — full evidence pass"
      - value: shallow
        label: "Shallow — minimal checks"
      - value: skip
        label: "Skip — no evidence"
    visibility: basic
  initial_build.webbing:
    label: "Webbing"
    help: "Run cross-node relationship discovery"
    widget: toggle
    visibility: advanced
  maintenance.model_tier:
    label: "Maintenance Tier"
    help: "Model tier used for ongoing pyramid updates"
    widget: select
    options_from: tier_registry
    visibility: basic
  maintenance.concurrency:
    label: "Maintenance Concurrency"
    help: "Parallel chain steps during maintenance"
    widget: number
    min: 1
    max: 16
    step: 1
    visibility: basic
  maintenance.evidence_mode:
    label: "Maintenance Evidence Mode"
    help: "How much evidence work to do during maintenance"
    widget: select
    options:
      - value: deep
        label: "Deep"
      - value: shallow
        label: "Shallow"
      - value: demand_only
        label: "Demand only"
      - value: skip
        label: "Skip"
    visibility: basic
  quality.min_distillation_length:
    label: "Min Distillation Length"
    help: "Characters required per distilled node"
    widget: number
    min: 0
    max: 10000
    step: 50
    suffix: "chars"
    visibility: advanced
  quality.require_evidence:
    label: "Require Evidence"
    help: "Reject nodes that land without evidence attached"
    widget: toggle
    visibility: advanced
  quality.require_webbing:
    label: "Require Webbing"
    help: "Reject nodes that land without webbing links"
    widget: toggle
    visibility: advanced
contributions.triggering_note: Bundled UI annotation for build_strategy (wire-node v0.2.0)

contributions.yaml_content:
schema_type: build_strategy
initial_build:
  model_tier: synth_heavy
  concurrency: 10
  evidence_mode: deep
  webbing: true
maintenance:
  model_tier: stale_local
  concurrency: 1
  evidence_mode: demand_only
  webbing: false
quality:
  min_distillation_length: 200
  require_evidence: true
  require_webbing: true
contributions.triggering_note: Bundled default build_strategy (wire-node v0.2.0)

contributions.yaml_content:
You are generating a DADBEAR policy YAML for a Wire Node knowledge pyramid.

DADBEAR is Wire Node's background auto-update loop: it watches a source
directory, debounces file changes, schedules maintenance scans, and propagates
staleness up the pyramid layers. The policy controls every knob — scan
intervals, debounce windows, session timeouts, batch sizes, propagation depth,
and the maintenance schedule (how aggressively DADBEAR keeps the pyramid fresh
when nothing is actively demanding answers).

Convert the user's natural-language intent into a valid `dadbear_policy` YAML
conforming to the JSON Schema below. Users typically describe trade-offs:
responsive vs quiet, aggressive vs lazy, frequent-batch vs big-batch.

## Schema (JSON Schema)
{schema}

## User Intent
{intent}

{if current_yaml}
## Current Values (you are refining this existing policy)
{current_yaml}
{end}

{if notes}
## User Refinement Notes
{notes}

Apply these notes to the current values. Keep everything else the same unless
the notes imply changes.
{end}

## Output rules

- Output ONLY the YAML document, no prose before or after
- Start the document with `schema_type: dadbear_policy`
- Intervals are in seconds (`scan_interval_secs`, `debounce_secs`,
  `session_timeout_secs`). Pick values that match the user's stated urgency —
  e.g. "responsive" implies single-digit `scan_interval_secs`, "quiet" implies
  tens to hundreds of seconds
- `maintenance_schedule.mode` is one of `always`, `demand_only`, `manual`
- Include inline comments that explain why a specific value was chosen when the
  intent directly drove it
contributions.triggering_note: Bundled generation prompt for dadbear_policy (wire-node v0.2.0)

contributions.yaml_content: {"$schema":"http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#","title":"DADBEAR Policy","type":"object","required":["schema_type"],"properties":{"schema_type":{"type":"string","const":"dadbear_policy"},"scan_interval_secs":{"type":"integer","minimum":1},"debounce_secs":{"type":"integer","minimum":0},"session_timeout_secs":{"type":"integer","minimum":60},"batch_size":{"type":"integer","minimum":1},"stale_propagation":{"type":"object","properties":{"max_cascade_depth":{"type":"integer","minimum":0},"propagation_model_tier":{"type":"string"}}},"maintenance_schedule":{"type":"object","properties":{"mode":{"type":"string","enum":["always","demand_only","manual"]},"check_interval":{"type":"string"},"model_tier":{"type":"string"}}}}}
contributions.triggering_note: Bundled JSON schema for dadbear_policy (wire-node v0.2.0)

contributions.yaml_content:
schema_type: dadbear_policy
scan_interval_secs: 30
debounce_secs: 60
session_timeout_secs: 1800
batch_size: 5
stale_propagation:
  max_cascade_depth: 3
  propagation_model_tier: stale_local
maintenance_schedule:
  mode: demand_only
  check_interval: "7d"
  model_tier: stale_local
contributions.triggering_note: Bundled default dadbear_policy (wire-node v0.2.0)

contributions.yaml_content:
You are generating a DADBEAR norms YAML for a Wire Node knowledge pyramid.

DADBEAR norms control the timing and threshold knobs for the background
auto-update loop — how often it scans, how long it debounces, when it promotes
sessions, how many files it batches, and when it considers a pyramid "runaway"
(too many stale nodes to rebuild safely). Norms are layered: a global default
applies to all pyramids, and per-pyramid overrides can tighten or loosen any
field.

Convert the user's natural-language intent into a valid `dadbear_norms` YAML
conforming to the JSON Schema below. Users typically describe trade-offs:
responsive vs quiet, aggressive vs lazy, frequent small batches vs large
batches, tight debounce vs generous debounce.

## Schema (JSON Schema)
{schema}

## User Intent
{intent}

{if current_yaml}
## Current Values (you are refining these existing norms)
{current_yaml}
{end}

{if notes}
## User Refinement Notes
{notes}

Apply these notes to the current values. Keep everything else the same unless
the notes imply changes.
{end}

## Output rules

- Output ONLY the YAML document, no prose before or after
- Start the document with `schema_type: dadbear_norms`
- Intervals are in seconds (`scan_interval_secs`, `debounce_secs`,
  `session_timeout_secs`). Pick values that match the user's stated urgency —
  e.g. "responsive" implies single-digit `scan_interval_secs`, "quiet" implies
  tens to hundreds of seconds
- `runaway_threshold` is a float between 0.0 and 1.0 — lower means more
  cautious (throttles earlier), higher means more aggressive (allows more
  staleness before throttling)
- `retention_window_days` is how long to keep old contribution versions —
  shorter saves disk, longer allows rollbacks
- `min_changed_files` controls the trigger threshold — 1 means every single
  file change triggers a rebuild, higher values batch changes together
- Include inline comments that explain why a specific value was chosen when the
  intent directly drove it
contributions.triggering_note: Bundled generation prompt for dadbear_norms (wire-node v0.2.0)

contributions.yaml_content: {"$schema":"http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#","title":"DADBEAR Norms","type":"object","required":["schema_type"],"properties":{"schema_type":{"type":"string","const":"dadbear_norms"},"scan_interval_secs":{"type":"integer","minimum":1,"default":10},"debounce_secs":{"type":"integer","minimum":1,"default":30},"session_timeout_secs":{"type":"integer","minimum":60,"default":1800},"batch_size":{"type":"integer","minimum":1,"default":1},"min_changed_files":{"type":"integer","minimum":1,"default":1},"runaway_threshold":{"type":"number","minimum":0.0,"maximum":1.0,"default":0.5},"retention_window_days":{"type":"integer","minimum":1,"default":30}}}
contributions.triggering_note: Bundled JSON schema for dadbear_norms (wire-node v0.2.0)

contributions.yaml_content:
schema_type: dadbear_norms
applies_to: dadbear_norms
version: 1
label: "DADBEAR Norms"
description: "Timing and threshold norms for DADBEAR scan behavior (global or per-pyramid)."
fields:
  scan_interval_secs:
    label: "Scan Interval"
    help: "How often DADBEAR polls the source folder for new or changed files (seconds)."
    widget: number
    min: 1
    max: 3600
    step: 1
    suffix: "sec"
    visibility: basic
    order: 1
  debounce_secs:
    label: "Debounce Window"
    help: "Seconds a file must be stable before a provisional build fires."
    widget: number
    min: 1
    max: 600
    step: 1
    suffix: "sec"
    visibility: basic
    order: 2
  session_timeout_secs:
    label: "Session Timeout"
    help: "Seconds of inactivity on a conversation file before session promotion."
    widget: number
    min: 60
    max: 7200
    step: 30
    suffix: "sec"
    visibility: advanced
    order: 3
  batch_size:
    label: "Batch Size"
    help: "How many pending ingest records DADBEAR dispatches per tick."
    widget: number
    min: 1
    max: 32
    step: 1
    visibility: basic
    order: 4
  min_changed_files:
    label: "Min Changed Files"
    help: "Minimum number of changed files before DADBEAR triggers a rebuild."
    widget: number
    min: 1
    max: 100
    step: 1
    visibility: advanced
    order: 5
  runaway_threshold:
    label: "Runaway Threshold"
    help: "Fraction of pyramid nodes that can be stale before throttling (0.0-1.0)."
    widget: number
    min: 0.0
    max: 1.0
    step: 0.05
    visibility: advanced
    order: 6
  retention_window_days:
    label: "Retention Window"
    help: "Days to keep superseded contribution versions before pruning."
    widget: number
    min: 1
    max: 365
    step: 1
    suffix: "days"
    visibility: advanced
    order: 7
contributions.triggering_note: Bundled UI annotation for dadbear_norms (wire-node v0.2.0)

contributions.yaml_content:

contributions.yaml_content:
You are generating a tier routing YAML for a Wire Node knowledge pyramid.

Tier routing maps abstract "model tiers" (e.g. `fast_extract`, `synth_heavy`,
`stale_local`) to concrete (provider, model) pairs. The pyramid executor asks
for tiers by name; the routing table resolves them to actual API targets with
per-token pricing. Users typically care about cost vs quality trade-offs and
local vs cloud preferences.

Convert the user's natural-language intent into a valid `tier_routing` YAML
conforming to the JSON Schema below. The generated tier list should cover
every tier the user mentions PLUS the standard Wire Node tiers (fast_extract,
synth_heavy, stale_local) so the executor never hits a missing-tier error.

## Schema (JSON Schema)
{schema}

## User Intent
{intent}

{if current_yaml}
## Current Values (you are refining this existing routing)
{current_yaml}
{end}

{if notes}
## User Refinement Notes
{notes}

Apply these notes to the current values. Keep the tiers the user didn't
mention the same unless the notes imply otherwise.
{end}

## Output rules

- Output ONLY the YAML document, no prose before or after
- Start the document with `schema_type: tier_routing`
- `entries` is a list of tier entries; each entry has `tier_name`,
  `provider_id`, `model_id`, and optional `priority`, `context_limit`,
  `prompt_price_per_token`, `completion_price_per_token`
- When the user references a named provider (e.g. "openrouter", "ollama",
  "anthropic"), use that as `provider_id`. Users manage their providers
  separately; the router does not need to validate them here
- Pricing fields are per TOKEN, not per 1M tokens; encode in scientific
  notation if needed (e.g. `3.0e-6`)
- Include inline comments when a tier choice reflects a specific intent signal
  (e.g. "# user asked for local-only fallback when cloud is unavailable")
contributions.triggering_note: Bundled generation prompt for tier_routing (wire-node v0.2.0)

contributions.yaml_content: {"$schema":"http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#","title":"Tier Routing","type":"object","required":["schema_type","entries"],"properties":{"schema_type":{"type":"string","const":"tier_routing"},"entries":{"type":"array","items":{"type":"object","required":["tier_name","provider_id","model_id"],"properties":{"tier_name":{"type":"string"},"provider_id":{"type":"string"},"model_id":{"type":"string"},"priority":{"type":"integer"},"context_limit":{"type":"integer","minimum":0},"prompt_price_per_token":{"type":"number","minimum":0},"completion_price_per_token":{"type":"number","minimum":0}}}}}}
contributions.triggering_note: Bundled JSON schema for tier_routing (wire-node v0.2.0)

contributions.yaml_content:
schema_type: tier_routing
entries:
  - tier_name: fast_extract
    provider_id: openrouter
    model_id: inception/mercury-2
    priority: 1
  - tier_name: synth_heavy
    provider_id: openrouter
    model_id: inception/mercury-2
    priority: 1
  - tier_name: stale_local
    provider_id: openrouter
    model_id: inception/mercury-2
    priority: 1
  - tier_name: high
    provider_id: openrouter
    model_id: qwen/qwen3.5-flash-02-23
    priority: 1
  - tier_name: max
    provider_id: openrouter
    model_id: x-ai/grok-4.20-beta
    priority: 1
contributions.triggering_note: Bundled default tier_routing (wire-node v0.2.0)

contributions.yaml_content:
You are generating a custom prompts YAML for a Wire Node knowledge pyramid.

Custom prompts steer what the pyramid extracts and synthesizes from its source
material. `extraction_focus` biases the chain's extractors toward particular
concepts; `synthesis_style` shapes how L1+ layer nodes phrase their
distillations; `vocabulary_priority` tells the clusterer which topic terms
should anchor the clustering; `ignore_patterns` marks material the extractors
should skip.

Convert the user's natural-language intent into a valid `custom_prompts` YAML
conforming to the JSON Schema below. The user's intent usually describes what
they care about in their source material (e.g. "track API design decisions",
"focus on user-visible behavior changes", "skip generated code").

## Schema (JSON Schema)
{schema}

## User Intent
{intent}

{if current_yaml}
## Current Values (you are refining this existing prompt set)
{current_yaml}
{end}

{if notes}
## User Refinement Notes
{notes}

Apply these notes to the current values. Keep unmentioned settings the same.
{end}

## Output rules

- Output ONLY the YAML document, no prose before or after
- Start the document with `schema_type: custom_prompts`
- `extraction_focus` and `synthesis_style` are free-text strings describing
  the desired extraction / synthesis bias. Keep them to 1-2 sentences each.
- `vocabulary_priority` is a list of topic terms, most-important first
- `ignore_patterns` is a list of textual patterns the extractor should ignore
  (regex-flavored but treated literally by the chain runtime)
- Include inline comments when the intent drives a specific choice
contributions.triggering_note: Bundled generation prompt for custom_prompts (wire-node v0.2.0)

contributions.yaml_content: {"$schema":"http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#","title":"Custom Prompts","type":"object","required":["schema_type"],"properties":{"schema_type":{"type":"string","const":"custom_prompts"},"extraction_focus":{"type":"string"},"synthesis_style":{"type":"string"},"vocabulary_priority":{"type":"array","items":{"type":"string"}},"ignore_patterns":{"type":"array","items":{"type":"string"}}}}
contributions.triggering_note: Bundled JSON schema for custom_prompts (wire-node v0.2.0)

contributions.yaml_content:
schema_type: custom_prompts
applies_to: custom_prompts
version: 1
label: "Custom Prompts"
description: "Steer what the pyramid extracts and synthesizes"
fields:
  extraction_focus:
    label: "Extraction Focus"
    help: "What the extractor should prioritize"
    widget: text
    visibility: basic
  synthesis_style:
    label: "Synthesis Style"
    help: "How L1+ nodes should phrase their distillations"
    widget: text
    visibility: basic
  vocabulary_priority:
    label: "Vocabulary Priority"
    help: "Topic terms anchoring the clusterer, most-important first"
    widget: list
    item_widget: text
    visibility: advanced
  ignore_patterns:
    label: "Ignore Patterns"
    help: "Patterns the extractor should skip"
    widget: list
    item_widget: text
    visibility: advanced
contributions.triggering_note: Bundled UI annotation for custom_prompts (wire-node v0.2.0)

contributions.yaml_content:
schema_type: custom_prompts
extraction_focus: "the structure and meaning of the source material"
synthesis_style: "concise, decision-oriented"
vocabulary_priority: []
ignore_patterns:
  - "boilerplate"
  - "generated code"
  - "test fixtures"
contributions.triggering_note: Bundled default custom_prompts (wire-node v0.2.0)

contributions.yaml_content:
schema_type: folder_ingestion_heuristics
# Phase 17 seed: defaults for recursive folder ingestion. Users can
# override any of these via a refined contribution on the Wire.
min_files_for_pyramid: 3
max_recursion_depth: 10
max_file_size_bytes: 10485760
default_scan_interval_secs: 30
code_extensions: [".rs", ".ts", ".tsx", ".py", ".go", ".js", ".jsx", ".java", ".rb", ".c", ".cpp", ".h", ".hpp", ".cs", ".swift", ".kt"]
document_extensions: [".md", ".txt", ".pdf", ".doc", ".docx", ".rst", ".org"]
# Ignore patterns. These supplement (but do NOT replace) the
# scanner's .gitignore / .pyramid-ignore walker. Kept in sync with
# `default_ignore_patterns()` in src-tauri/src/pyramid/db.rs.
#
# Pattern semantics:
#   name/   → matches any path component named 'name' at any depth
#   *.ext   → case-insensitive basename suffix match
#   bare    → exact basename, component, or substring on the full path
ignore_patterns:
  # Build artifacts and vendored deps
  - "node_modules/"
  - "target/"
  - ".git/"
  - "dist/"
  - "build/"
  - "out/"
  - ".next/"
  - ".nuxt/"
  - ".turbo/"
  - "coverage/"
  - ".nyc_output/"
  # Language-specific caches
  - "__pycache__/"
  - ".pytest_cache/"
  - ".mypy_cache/"
  - ".ruff_cache/"
  - ".venv/"
  - "venv/"
  # Editor / IDE state
  - ".idea/"
  - ".vscode/"
  # Alternate VCS
  - ".svn/"
  - ".hg/"
  # AI agent session state (Claude Code conversations are ingested
  # separately via claude_code_conversation_path, not as source docs)
  - ".claude/"
  # Filesystem oddities: timestamped experiment backups and literal
  # '~/' dirs from shell-escape mishaps
  - ".lab.bak."
  - "~/"
  # Files
  - "*.lock"
  - "*.bin"
  - "*.exe"
  - "*.dylib"
  - ".DS_Store"
respect_gitignore: true
respect_pyramid_ignore: true
vine_collapse_single_child: true
claude_code_auto_include: true
claude_code_conversation_path: "~/.claude/projects"
contributions.triggering_note: Bundled default folder_ingestion_heuristics (wire-node v0.2.0)

contributions.yaml_content:
schema_type: wire_discovery_weights
fields:
  # Wire discovery ranking weights — tuned for trust quality signals,
  # penalize staleness, but don't let network effects dominate. See
  # docs/specs/wire-discovery-ranking.md for the full rationale.
  w_rating: 0.25
  w_adoption: 0.20
  w_freshness: 0.15
  w_chain: 0.10
  w_reputation: 0.10
  w_challenge: 0.10
  w_internalization: 0.10
contributions.triggering_note: Bundled default wire_discovery_weights (wire-node v0.2.0)

contributions.yaml_content:
schema_type: wire_auto_update_settings
# Per-schema_type auto-update toggles. All disabled by default —
# users opt in per category. Auto-pulls are refused for any
# contribution that introduces new credential references.
auto_update:
  dadbear_policy: false
  evidence_policy: false
  build_strategy: false
  tier_routing: false
  custom_prompts: false
  step_overrides: false
  folder_ingestion_heuristics: false
  custom_chains: false
  skill: false
  schema_definition: false
  schema_annotation: false
  wire_discovery_weights: false
  dadbear_norms: false
contributions.triggering_note: Bundled default wire_auto_update_settings (wire-node v0.2.0)

contributions.yaml_content:
schema_type: wire_update_polling
# Interval between supersession-check polls in seconds. Default is
# 21600 (6 hours) — the Wire doesn't change very fast and we'd
# rather let updates arrive on the next cycle than waste bandwidth.
interval_secs: 21600
contributions.triggering_note: Bundled default wire_update_polling (wire-node v0.2.0)

contributions.yaml_content: {"$schema":"http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#","title":"Compute Participation Policy","type":"object","required":["schema_type","mode"],"properties":{"schema_type":{"type":"string","const":"compute_participation_policy"},"mode":{"type":"string","enum":["coordinator","hybrid","worker"]},"allow_fleet_dispatch":{"type":"boolean","description":"Send work to fleet peers. Null means project from mode."},"allow_fleet_serving":{"type":"boolean","description":"Accept work from fleet peers. Null means project from mode."},"allow_market_dispatch":{"type":"boolean","description":"Send work to the compute market. Null means project from mode."},"allow_market_visibility":{"type":"boolean","description":"Publish compute offers + accept compute market jobs. Null means project from mode."},"allow_storage_pulling":{"type":"boolean","description":"Initiate document pulls from the storage market. Null means project from mode."},"allow_storage_hosting":{"type":"boolean","description":"Publish storage offers + host documents. Null means project from mode."},"allow_relay_usage":{"type":"boolean","description":"Request relay chains for own dispatch. Null means project from mode."},"allow_relay_serving":{"type":"boolean","description":"Accept relay forwards from the network. Null means project from mode."},"allow_serving_while_degraded":{"type":"boolean","description":"Accept work when health_status=degraded. Operator-configurable, never projected from mode."}}}
contributions.triggering_note: Bundled JSON schema for compute_participation_policy v2 (10-field DD-I canonical — wire-node Phase 2 WS1a)

contributions.yaml_content:
schema_type: compute_participation_policy
# Default per operational purpose lock
# (docs/plans/call-model-unified-market-integration.md §Purpose):
# a GPU-less tester should build a pyramid via the network without
# seeing a market word. That demands compute-market requester be
# on-by-default for fresh installs.
#
# Persisted explicit operator overrides ALWAYS win via
# effective_booleans unwrap_or(projection) — this seed only fires
# when no persisted contribution exists.
#
# Null values on the projected booleans let the mode's projection
# apply naturally. Persisted explicit `false` still takes precedence.
mode: hybrid
# Fleet: participate (matches shipped default).
allow_fleet_dispatch: true
allow_fleet_serving: true
# Compute market: follow mode projection (Hybrid → true for dispatch +
# visibility). Fresh installs get cooperative network on; operators
# can disable per-capability in Settings.
allow_market_dispatch: null
allow_market_visibility: null
# Storage market: off until operator opts in (S1, not yet shipped).
allow_storage_pulling: false
allow_storage_hosting: false
# Relay market: off until operator opts in (R1, not yet shipped).
allow_relay_usage: false
allow_relay_serving: false
# Operational safety: off by default.
allow_serving_while_degraded: false
# Phase 3 compute-market dispatch wall-clock (walker's max_wait on
# market entries). The queue-depth threshold and eager flag were
# retired in walker-re-plan-wire-2.1 Wave 5 — the walker attempts
# /quote on every market entry and advances on saturation.
market_dispatch_max_wait_ms: 900000
# Rev 2.1.1 saturation-retry patience (wall-clock budget per chunk for
# walker's saturation-backoff loop on all_offers_saturated_for_model).
# Default 3600s for batch builds (slow-and-steady through the market).
# Interactive workflows should supersede to ~60s so user-visible waits
# fall through to fallback faster.
market_saturation_patience_secs: 3600
contributions.triggering_note: Bundled default compute_participation_policy v2 (10-field DD-I canonical — wire-node Phase 2 WS1a)

Written by migration code as a superseding contribution; read at boot to decide whether a migration pass is needed.","type":"object","required":["schema_type","body"],"properties":{"schema_type":{"type":"string","const":"migration_marker"},"body":{"type":"string","description":"Schema-version string (e.g. 'v2', 'v3-db-migrated-config-pending', 'v3')"}}}

applies_to: migration_marker

label: "Migration Marker"

  body:
    label: "Schema Version"
    help: "Current schema version string (e.g. v2, v3-db-migrated-config-pending, v3). Managed by migration code; do not edit manually."
    widget: text
    visibility: advanced
contributions.triggering_note: Bundled UI annotation for migration_marker (walker v3 Phase 0a-2)

contributions.yaml_content:
You are generating a migration_marker YAML for a Wire Node.

The migration_marker is an internal schema-version tracker — its body is a
version string like `v2`, `v3-db-migrated-config-pending`, or `v3`. It is
written by migration code at boot when schema versions change, NOT by
operators. Operator-generated migration_marker YAMLs are rare; this skill
exists to complete the four-part bundle for the schema but typically will
not be invoked.

## Schema (JSON Schema)
{schema}

## User Intent
{intent}

## Output rules

- Output ONLY the YAML document, no prose before or after
- Start the document with `schema_type: migration_marker`
- Set `body` to the version string the user requests (verbatim)
- Do not invent intermediate version strings; use exactly what the user provides
contributions.triggering_note: Bundled generation prompt for migration_marker (walker v3 Phase 0a-2)

contributions.yaml_content:
schema_type: migration_marker
# Pre-walker-v3 state. Walker v3 migration logic supersedes this with
# `v3-db-migrated-config-pending` mid-transaction, then `v3` after the
# post-DDL config-file rewrite completes. Missing marker is treated as
# `v2` per the boot rules in walker-provider-configs-and-slot-policy-v3
# §2.18 / §6.
body: "v2"
contributions.triggering_note: Bundled default migration_marker (walker v3 Phase 0a-2)

contributions.yaml_content: {"$schema":"http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#","title":"Onboarding State","description":"Durable onboarding progress and acknowledgments. Written by the onboarding save path, Page 4 market consent, unknown-provider migration acks, and chain-engine-enable acks. Surfaces in Settings as the authoritative source of completed onboarding steps.","type":"object","required":["schema_type"],"properties":{"schema_type":{"type":"string","const":"onboarding_state"},"onboarding_complete_at":{"type":["string","null"],"description":"ISO-8601 timestamp of first full onboarding completion. Null until operator finishes the flow."},"completed_pages":{"type":"array","items":{"type":"string"},"description":"Page identifiers the operator has completed (e.g. identity, storage, mesh, market-consent)."},"migration_acks":{"type":"object","additionalProperties":{"type":"boolean"},"description":"Per-migration acknowledgments keyed by migration id (e.g. migration_unknown_providers_ack). True once operator has acknowledged the prompt."},"chain_engine_enable_ack":{"type":["boolean","null"],"description":"Operator acknowledgment of chain-engine enable (A-F5 / use_chain_engine explicit-false modal)."},"re_onboarding_required":{"type":"boolean","default":false,"description":"Set true by restore-from-backup when node identity mismatch is detected; Settings surfaces a re-onboarding prompt."}}}
contributions.triggering_note: Bundled JSON schema for onboarding_state (walker v3 Phase 0a-2)

contributions.yaml_content:
schema_type: schema_annotation
applies_to: onboarding_state
version: 1
label: "Onboarding State"
description: "Durable onboarding progress and migration acknowledgments."
# operator_private: syncs to Wire as authenticated-private (restorable across
# reinstalls), unlike local_only which never leaves the node.
operator_private: true
fields:
  onboarding_complete_at:
    label: "Onboarding Completed At"
    help: "Timestamp of first full onboarding completion"
    widget: text
    visibility: basic
  completed_pages:
    label: "Completed Pages"
    help: "Onboarding page ids the operator has finished"
    widget: list
    item_widget: text
    visibility: basic
  migration_acks:
    label: "Migration Acknowledgments"
    help: "Per-migration ack flags keyed by migration id"
    widget: code
    visibility: advanced
  chain_engine_enable_ack:
    label: "Chain Engine Enable Ack"
    help: "Operator acknowledgment for use_chain_engine enable (A-F5 modal)."
    widget: toggle
    visibility: advanced
  re_onboarding_required:
    label: "Re-onboarding Required"
    help: "Set true when a restore-from-backup detected node identity mismatch"
    widget: toggle
    visibility: advanced
contributions.triggering_note: Bundled UI annotation for onboarding_state (walker v3 Phase 0a-2)

contributions.yaml_content:
You are generating an onboarding_state YAML for a Wire Node.

The onboarding_state contribution tracks durable onboarding progress —
when onboarding completed, which pages were finished, which migration
prompts were acknowledged, whether the chain-engine enable modal was
acknowledged, and whether a restore-from-backup detected identity
mismatch. This contribution is written by the onboarding save path, Page
4 market-consent, the unknown-provider migration modal, and the
chain-engine-enable ack path — NOT by operators directly. This generation
skill exists to complete the four-part bundle; typical use is narrow
(e.g. manual repair flows).

## Schema (JSON Schema)
{schema}

## User Intent
{intent}

## Output rules

- Output ONLY the YAML document, no prose before or after
- Start the document with `schema_type: onboarding_state`
- Use null / absent fields where the user's intent does not set them
- Do not fabricate timestamps; if the user doesn't supply one, omit
  `onboarding_complete_at`
contributions.triggering_note: Bundled generation prompt for onboarding_state (walker v3 Phase 0a-2)

contributions.yaml_content:
schema_type: onboarding_state
# Uninitialized — no onboarding completion, no pages completed, no acks.
# Walker v3 onboarding save path / Page 4 consent / unknown-provider
# migration modal / chain-engine-enable ack path all supersede this seed
# with operator-written state.
onboarding_complete_at: null
completed_pages: []
migration_acks: {}
chain_engine_enable_ack: null
re_onboarding_required: false
contributions.triggering_note: Bundled default onboarding_state (walker v3 Phase 0a-2)

contributions.yaml_content: {"$schema":"http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#","title":"Node Identity History","description":"Current node identity plus the rotation history. On re-onboarding, the rotation flow supersedes this with the new current + previous appended. The SelfDealing check (walker v3 §2.16.7) reads the active contribution to recognize this node's own historic identities. operator_private: historic node_ids survive reinstall via Wire authenticated-private sync (distinct from local_only which never leaves the node — see §5.4.3).","type":"object","required":["schema_type"],"properties":{"schema_type":{"type":"string","const":"node_identity_history"},"current":{"type":["string","null"],"description":"Current NodeId. Null only in the bundled seed before first identity provision."},"history":{"type":"array","items":{"type":"object","required":["node_id","rotated_at","reason"],"properties":{"node_id":{"type":"string"},"rotated_at":{"type":"string","description":"ISO-8601 timestamp of when this node_id was rotated out of `current`"},"reason":{"type":"string","description":"Free-text rotation reason (e.g. re_onboarding, reinstall, key_compromise)"}}}}}}
contributions.triggering_note: Bundled JSON schema for node_identity_history (walker v3 Phase 0a-2)

contributions.yaml_content:
schema_type: schema_annotation
applies_to: node_identity_history
version: 1
label: "Node Identity History"
description: "Current node identity plus rotation history. Read by the SelfDealing check."
# operator_private: syncs to Wire as authenticated-private so historic
# node_ids survive reinstall. Distinct from local_only (which never
# leaves the node). See walker-provider-configs-and-slot-policy-v3 §5.4.3.
operator_private: true
fields:
  current:
    label: "Current Node ID"
    help: "This node's current identity. Null only in the bundled seed before first provisioning."
    widget: text
    visibility: basic
  history:
    label: "Rotation History"
    help: "Prior node_ids with timestamps and reasons. Appended on every re-onboarding rotation."
    widget: code
    visibility: advanced
contributions.triggering_note: Bundled UI annotation for node_identity_history (walker v3 Phase 0a-2)

contributions.yaml_content:
You are generating a node_identity_history YAML for a Wire Node.

This contribution tracks the node's current identity plus its rotation
history. It is written by the identity-provisioning and re-onboarding
rotation flows, NOT by operators directly. This generation skill exists
to complete the four-part bundle; typical use is narrow (e.g. manual
repair flows after a restore).

## Schema (JSON Schema)
{schema}

## User Intent
{intent}

## Output rules

- Output ONLY the YAML document, no prose before or after
- Start the document with `schema_type: node_identity_history`
- Do NOT fabricate node_ids; only emit values the user supplies verbatim
- `rotated_at` must be ISO-8601; copy verbatim from user intent
- `reason` is free text; use the user's wording (e.g. re_onboarding,
  reinstall, key_compromise)
contributions.triggering_note: Bundled generation prompt for node_identity_history (walker v3 Phase 0a-2)

contributions.yaml_content:
schema_type: node_identity_history
# Empty seed — current identity is set by the provisioning flow, not the
# bundled default. SelfDealing check treats `current: null` + empty
# history as "no prior identity to match against" and falls through.
current: null
history: []
contributions.triggering_note: Bundled default node_identity_history (walker v3 Phase 0a-2)

contributions.yaml_content:
You are migrating a configuration YAML from one schema version to another.

A Wire Node configuration is a YAML document validated against a JSON Schema. When the schema for a configuration type evolves (a field is added, removed, renamed, or has its type/constraints changed), every existing YAML document for that schema_type may need updating to remain valid against the new schema. Your job: take the user's existing YAML and produce a new YAML that is valid against the new schema while preserving the user's original intent as faithfully as possible.

This is NOT a regeneration. The user already made deliberate choices when they wrote the existing YAML. Honor those choices wherever the new schema still accepts them. Only change what the schema change forces you to change.

## Old schema (the user's current YAML below was valid against this)
{old_schema}

## New schema (the migrated YAML must be valid against this)
{new_schema}

## User's current YAML (against the old schema)
{old_yaml}

{if user_note}
## User guidance for the migration
{user_note}
{end}

## Migration rules

- Preserve every value the user explicitly set in the old YAML, AS LONG AS the new schema still accepts that value at that path
- For fields that exist in both schemas with compatible types, copy the value through unchanged
- For fields that were RENAMED (same semantic meaning, new key name), copy the value to the new key
- For fields that were REMOVED from the new schema, drop them silently
- For fields that are NEW in the new schema and required, add them with the most conservative reasonable default that fits the user's apparent intent from the rest of the YAML — never invent values out of thin air
- For fields whose TYPE changed, coerce the value only if the coercion is lossless (int → float is fine; "yes" → true is fine; an array of strings → a single string IS NOT fine)
- For fields whose ENUM values changed, map the old value to the closest equivalent if one exists, otherwise drop and note in a comment
- For fields whose CONSTRAINT tightened (e.g. min_value increased), clamp the user's value to the new bound and note the clamp in a comment

## Output rules

- Output ONLY the migrated YAML document, no prose before or after
- Use inline YAML comments (`# ...`) for EVERY non-trivial transformation so the user can review what changed and why
- Comment format: `# migrated: <what changed and why>`
- Examples:
    - `# migrated: renamed from 'batch_size' to 'concurrency' in v2 schema`
    - `# migrated: dropped — field no longer exists in new schema`
    - `# migrated: clamped from 32 to 16 (new max)`
    - `# migrated: defaulted to 'review' — new required field, no signal in old YAML`
- Start the document with `schema_type: <type>` matching the new schema's required value
- Keep the YAML structurally minimal — do NOT add fields that were not in the old YAML and are not required by the new schema
- The output must parse as valid YAML and validate against the new schema
contributions.triggering_note: Bundled migration prompt for config schema migration (wire-node v0.2.0)
